rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a material internal inconsistency: the stimuli are described only as verbal/qualitative phrases ('high confidence', 'fairly sure'), yet the results compare 'numeric phrases' against 'qualitative phrases' — a condition never introduced in the methods. This logical gap could be load-bearing if methodological checking is the intended task.
rationale.R1.3: Three result claims (mixed-effects model outcome, direction of trust-rating effect, inter-rater agreement on free-text coding) require alignment with the described design; whether that alignment check is load-bearing depends on the intended scope of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.6: The artifact exhibits tense inconsistencies within a single sentence ('completes' vs. 'were asked'), sustained run-on sentence constructions, and prose-level redundancy; writing polish would be load-bearing if the delegation targets surface quality rather than methodological soundness.
